data.title: Taiwan Semi surges after 'blowout quarter' and outlook keeps Wall Street upbeat
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor's (NYSE:TSM) stock soared about 13% on Thursday after third-quarter results and guidance beat estimates, drawing positive reactions from Wall Street.

Several chip stocks were in the green following TSM's upbeat performance. Nvidia (NVDA) +3%, Qualcomm (QCOM) +2%, Broadcom (AVGO) +4%, AMD (AMD) and Intel (INTC) +1%, each, Micron Technology (MU) +3%.

Needham kept Buy rating and $210 price target on the stock.

"TSMC reported a blowout quarter with revenue, gross margin, and operating margin results and guidance that well exceeded consensus estimates," said analysts led by Charles Shi.

Taiwan Semi now expects 2024 CapEx to be slightly higher than $30B and even higher in 2025. However, to the analysts this seems to be $1B trim to this year's CapEx budget.

The analysts said that due to relatively downbeat quarterly CapEx numbers for the first three quarters of the year (at around $6B per quarter), the CapEx guidance implies that in the fourth quarter of 2024 it will almost double and reach about $12B, likely driven by the initial N2 buildout.

TSM's 3nm process technology contributed 20% of total wafer revenue, while the 5nm process tech accounted for 32% of total wafer revenue.

The analysts said that by node, relative to Needham's estimates, TSM's third quarter results show much stronger performance of N3 and slightly weaker performance of N5. The analysts think the N3 upside should be mainly driven by Apple, while N5 revenue seems to be leveling off after six consecutive quarters of strong sequential growth.

Chairman and CEO C.C. Wei said on the company's earnings call that revenue contribution from server AI processors will more than triple this year and account for a mid-teens percentage of total revenue in 2024.

Based on TSM's management's commentary, the analysts estimate that the company's AI revenue was around $4B last year, and will likely reach $13B this year.

TSM has guided AI to account for 20% of the total revenue by 2028, which would result in around over $33B if the 15%+ total revenue CAGR continues through the next four years, according to the analysts.

Shi and his team said that TSM's reported revenue split between technology nodes suggests that the non-wafer revenue, the majority of which comes from packaging, saw a meaningful step-up to $3.2B in the third quarter of 2024 from $2.4B in the second quarter of 2024, a 36% increase.

This could be partially driven by Apple's ramp that drives InFO revenue, but the magnitude of the sequential growth appears to be above seasonality (around 20% quarter-over-quarter) and does support the rumor that TSM may have released 60% to 80% more Chip on Wafer on Substrate, or CoWoS, capacity in the third quarter of 2024.

BofA also maintained a Buy rating on TSM's stock.

The analysts raised the price target on the stock to NT$1,400 (35% upside potential) to reflect the company's strong third quarter results and fourth quarter guidance, better AI strength, and solid industry leadership.

The sustained 2024 capex and upbeat tone on 2025 capex further support this outlook, the analysts added.

The analysts noted that the upbeat fourth quarter guidance has strengthened their confidence in future growth.

Valuation remains attractive helped by advances in Al, including Nvidia's (NVDA) Blackwell's ramp-up, improved N3 yields, rising demand for advanced nodes, average selling price hikes, and market-share gains, all of which are set to drive structural earnings growth, according to the analysts.

The analysts added that they have reiterated the Buy rating despite recent demand concerns.

The demand outlook is positive, with strong Al demand marking the start of a long-term trend. Non-Al segments are stabilizing and recovering. PC and smartphone (around 45% to 50% revenue) are benefiting from faster silicon area growth, the analysts stated.

Beyond 2026, growth prospects remain robust, with high demand for N2, A16 technologies, and TSM's tech leadership. The analysts expect competition to ease continuously. TSM sees sizable orders from the U.S. Integrated Device Manufacturers, or IDMs, still and shows no interest in acquiring fabs from the latter, the analysts added.

BofA sees limited risk of anti-trust issues, as the company holds only about 30% industry share under the foundry 2.0 Total addressable market, or TAM, and its market behavior does not fit the criteria for antitrust concerns.

The slower ASML shipments could signal improved long-term foundry supply/demand, further strengthening TSM's market position, according to the analysts.

data.title: Blackstone is preparing to exit some investments via IPOs - report
data.description:
• A technology-led rally in global stocks markets is leading Blackstone (NYSE:BX) to start the wheels turning on taking some of its biggest investments public through initial public offerings, according to a media report.
  • Blackstone (BX) President Jonathan Gray told the Financial Times that the private equity firm is "preparing to take some portfolio companies public... I would say the discussions have gone from theoretical to practical."
  • The plans have progressed to the point where the firm is talking about timing, he added. The potential IPOs, though, hinge on stable equity markets.
  • Some of the firm's larger investments in recent years include Merlin Entertainments, which owns Legoland, U.S. health supplies company Medline Industries, and UKG, a UK payrolls software firm.
  • Limited opportunities to exit its investments have weighed on Blackstone's (BX) realized performance fees. Realized performance revenue for the first nine months of 2024 of rose 4% Y/Y to $1.37B from a depressed level in 2023, but was far lower than $3.35B in the first nine months of 2022.
  • Blackstone (BX) stock jumped 4.8% in Thursday morning trading after it increased its quarterly dividend by 5% and posted stronger-than-expected Q3 earnings.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow rise after robust results from TSMC and strong retail sales
data.description:
The major averages were higher on Thursday, with Nasdaq leading the pack, after strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and robust retail sales, helped revive investor sentiments.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.2%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

U.S. Treasury yields climbed as investors sold off bonds following stronger-than-expected September retail sales data. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.08%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.99%.

Initial jobless claims matched and fell to 241K in the past week, while U.S. retail sales climbed more than expected in September. The Philly Fed outlook came in at +10.3 in October.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended higher, rebounding from a rout in chip stocks in the previous session. An ongoing rotation into small-caps and other sectors buoyed markets, while financials surged on a post-earnings pop in Morgan Stanley (MS).

"Markets put in a solid performance yesterday, with investors remaining upbeat as they digested several earnings results and another modest decline in oil prices," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) shares were up 12.7%, after it beat both its Q3 bottom and top line as it continued to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom, and helped traders renew their confidence in tech stocks.

Also, U.S. industrial production numbers retreat more than expected in September as levels dipped 0.3% M/M. Also on the economic docket, the Fed balance sheet is expected to land later in the day.

"Manufacturing output (on the official data) has risen slightly this year. Sentiment (ISM, Philly Fed) has pointed to a near continuous contraction for the past two years. It is enough to make one question whether sentiment lives in the real world," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: ETFs with heavy weightings in Taiwan Semi catch a ride higher
data.description:
Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) have soared on Thursday morning after the world's largest contract chipmaker delivered strong earnings, which in turn pushed many exchange-traded funds higher as TSM is popular among ETFs.

Of the 83 ETFs that hold TSM, nine of the names have portfolio allocations towards TSM of 10% or greater. All nine funds find themselves higher on the day thanks in part to TSM. Outlined below are the nine funds along with each of their allocations towards TSM. The list is ranked by weighting percentages.

No. 9: Global X Emerging Markets Great Consumer ETF (EMC), 10.00% weighting.

No. 8: Wahed Dow Jones Islamic World ETF (UMMA), 10.79% weighting.

No. 7: Freedom 100 Emerging Markets ETF (FRDM), 11.43% weighting.

No. 6: Matthews Pacific Tiger Active ETF (ASIA), 11.60% weighting.

No. 5: National Security Emerging Markets Index ETF (NSI), 12.50% weighting.

No. 4: VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH), 12.88% weighting.

No. 3: Matthews Emerging Markets Ex China Active ETF (MEMX), 14.37% weighting.

No. 2: SP Funds S&P Global Technology ETF (SPTE), 15.54% weighting.

No. 1: SP Funds S&P World ETF (SPWO), 16.43% weighting.

For reference, shares of TSM are higher by 11.1% after the global foundry reported third-quarter results and guidance that surpassed expectations, amid increased demand for artificial intelligence chips.

"Our business in the third quarter was supported by strong smartphone and AI-related demand for our industry-leading 3nm and 5nm technologies," said TSM's Senior VP and CFO Wendell Huang.

data.title: Alcoa turns lower despite swinging to Q3 profit on higher alumina prices
data.description:
Alcoa (NYSE:AA) -1.2% in early trading Thursday following a surge of as much as 10% pre-market after easily beating expectations for Q3 adjusted earnings, which analysts said were aided by surging alumina prices, and reaching a proposed agreement to secure the viability of its San Ciprian, Spain, alumina and aluminum plant.

Q3 net income swung to $90M, or $0.38/share, from a loss of $168M, or a $0.94/share loss, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenues rose 11.6% Y/Y to $2.9B.

Q3 alumina production fell 4% Q/Q to 2.44M metric tons primarily due to the full curtailment of the Kwinana refinery completed in June 2024, while aluminum segment production rose 3% to 559K metric tons primarily due to continued progress on the Alumar smelter restart.

Q3 third-party alumina shipments fell 9% Q/Q primarily due to decreased trading, and total aluminum shipments declined 6% primarily due to decreased trading and the timing of shipments.

Alcoa (AA) anticipates improved performance in its alumina segment in Q4, CFO Molly Beerman said on its earnings conference call, forecasting FY 2024 alumina segment shipments will reach 12.9M-13.1M metric tons, up 200K tons from previous projections due to increased trading volumes.

Alcoa's (AA) Q3 results were solid, as alumina tightness is a positive for the near-term outlook, and the San Ciprian partnership could lead to a favorable resolution, J.P. Morgan's Bill Peterson said, raising his stock price target to $39 from $36 while maintaining a Neutral rating.

data.title: Jefferies’ updated ‘Franchise picks’: CPAY, KVUE, SAIA, PFGC
data.description:
Jefferies analysts updated their “Franchise Picks” with four new additions and four subtractions.

These are 21 of Jefferies’ highest-conviction, buy-rated U.S. stocks that have valuation levels that suggest upside.

Analysts added Corpay (CPAY), Kenvue (KVUE), Performance Food Group (PFGC), and Saia (SAIA), and removed PepsiCo (PEP), STAG Industrial (STAG), Sysco Corp. (SYY), and Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp. (WAB).

Since its inception, the Jefferies Franchise Pick list has reached almost 280% total return, compared to about 270% for the S&P 500 (SP500).

Here is the list, with their 2025 EPS estimates by Jefferies analysts:

  • Amgen Inc. (AMGN) – Price target: $380.00; 2025 EPS: 20.78

data.title: Macy's launches holiday collection featuring Mickey and Minnie Mouse
data.description:
Macy’s (NYSE:M) is collaborating with Disney (NYSE:DIS) this holiday season with limited edition Mickey and Minnie Mouse merchandise, along with the debut of a Minnie Mouse character balloon at the Macy’s (M) Thanksgiving Day Parade.

The Mickey and Minnie collection for kids and adults will feature Disney (DIS) artwork by Jeff Shelly, Disney’s legendary director of character art.

“Macy’s and Disney have created magic together for ninety years, establishing a legacy that is beloved across multiple generations,” said Nata Dvir, chief merchandising officer at Macy’s. “We recognize this rich history and are excited to offer our customers this exclusive holiday assortment.”

Beginning October 17 through the New Year, Macy’s Herald Square flagship store and 200 additional stores across the country will have a dedicated Mickey and Minnie Shop with Herald Square hosting a special concession stand and interactive AR experience. LEGO will also host customer build events in select Macy’s (M) stores on Saturday, November 2.

The publicity and festivities surrounding the Mickey and Minnie collection hope to breathe life into the struggling department store which recently said it would hire fewer employees this holiday season, attributed to the softer consumer environment, inflation, and what is expected to be the slowest holiday sales season in six years.

Shares are up 1.5% on Thursday for a third consecutive day in the green.
